body_txt: Throughout most people's lives, they'll be told by at least one authority figure, typically a parent, that violence is never the answer. However, in many situations, violence tends to get more attention than trying to be reasonable. Then there's the character who combines the two. They will use violence, but only in situations where it's absolutely necessary. They might be perfectly capable of killing, but for either moral or pragmatic reasons, prefer not to do so unless left with absolutely no choice. And even then, they'll often make killing their last resort, trusting in the judicious application of pain or injury to stop their opponents instead. They could be a Combat Pragmatist , Genius Bruiser , The Soft-Hearted Warrior , Badass Preacher , or any combination thereof. They are also those who tend to be very good at Talking the Monster to Death or using a Civilized Threat . However, while they are judicious in their application of violence, once they decide that it is necessary, it does not tend to pay to be their adversary . They often reveal in the most unpleasant of ways that not liking violence is not the same thing as not being skilled at using it. For a variety of reasons, this mindset was common to certain genres, especially the comic book Superhero , who were presented as strong and empowered, but having to present as upstanding and moral. As such, they were allowed to use violence only as befitting the threat before them. This led to complaints about villains always returning from their Cardboard Prisons , and would eventually lead to the rise of the Anti-Hero characters who had no qualms about putting down foes for good. Of course, even reasonable people can be pushed too far, so if a character who is judicious in their application of violence is pushed too far, it's very likely to become a case of Beware the Nice Ones . Compare Technical Pacifist , for characters who are willing to fight but prefer disabling their opponents non-lethally to killing them, if possible. Also compare Thou Shalt Not Kill , wherein non-lethal violence is fine, but killing is not. Unlike Violence Is the Only Option , where all other alternatives have proven useless, this trope can be in effect if someone who has used violence in the past, or will in the future, is capable of avoiding it when a situation doesn't necessitate it. Also, when the situation absolutely requires violence, only the bare minimum is applied, as opposed to resorting to There Is No Kill Like Overkill . Contrast No-Holds-Barred Beatdown , There Is No Kill Like Overkill , and Curb-Stomp Battle , wherein overwhelming force is brought to bear. Also contrast Does Not Know His Own Strength , where someone applies too much force without intending to, as well as Disproportionate Retribution , when someone applies far more force than fits the crime. Contrast Suicidal Pacifism where someone shuns violence even when the situation warrants it, even at the cost of their own lives or the lives of others.
